text.title: Role Cheatsheet Generator Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every cheatsheet-generator run must enforce so the artefact is reproducible, tier-safe, and teaches a single canonical path per role.

rule.statement: For a fixed (corpus-version, role, tier) input, the generator must produce byte-identical output across runs. Ordering uses a documented tie-breaker chain: priority signal desc → role-tag-match-count desc → slug alphabetical.
rule.rationale: Non-deterministic output makes diffs across corpus releases unreviewable. The fixed tie-breaker chain also removes the temptation to "shuffle the list to look fresh".

rule.statement: The generator must read tier-manifest.json and filter out any methodology whose tier is above the requested org tier. A cheatsheet referencing pro-tier content for a solo-tier org is rejected at generate-time, not at click-time.
rule.rationale: A cheatsheet that links to content the team can't open trains the team to ignore the cheatsheet. Filtering at generate-time also means the artefact can be cached as a static file.

rule.statement: Role membership is determined exclusively by the `role:` tag list in each methodology's frontmatter. The generator must never infer role from slug substrings, group folder, or summary text.
rule.rationale: Heuristic role inference produces silent miscategorisation (a methodology slugged "architect-review-template" is not necessarily architect-only). The tag-only rule forces missing tags to be fixed at the source, not patched in the generator.

rule.statement: The generated file carries a `# generated-do-not-edit` header with corpus-version, role, tier, and timestamp. Manual edits to the file body are not supported and will be overwritten on the next regenerate. Curated overrides live in a separate `cheatsheet-<role>.overrides.md` file that the generator merges.
rule.rationale: Edits-in-place cause divergence between corpus and cheatsheet that the next regenerate silently wipes. The separate overrides file lets teams pin must-include items without breaking the regenerate-from-scratch property.

rule.statement: The cheatsheet is capped at 10 primary entries plus up to 5 overrides. Requests for N>10 are rejected with a hint to split into multiple role-scoped cheatsheets (e.g. architect-runtime, architect-data) rather than grow one list.
rule.rationale: A "top 30" is not a cheatsheet, it is another index. The hard cap forces the priority signal to actually rank, and forces overflow to become its own narrower cheatsheet rather than diluting the canonical one.

rule.statement: If role-tag coverage <80% for the target role OR the role has <10 distinct methodologies OR an org already maintains a curated cheatsheet, STOP.
rule.rationale: Skip applies when generator would teach the wrong defaults or duplicate human curation.

example.description: Generator invocation and resulting top-of-file header.
example.code: <!-- generated-do-not-edit corpus_version: 2026-05-20 role: software-architect tier: pro top_n: 10 overrides_merged: 2 --> # Software Architect — Top 10 Faion Methodologies 1. pro/infra/greenfield-infra-decision-matrix 2. pro/infra/capacity-planning-at-design-time ...
